content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 12, 1977

A-97294

William P. Hopkins
City Attorney
City of Anaheim
P.O. Box 3222
Anaheim, CA 92803

Re: Opinion Request No. 77-005

Dear Mr. Hopkins:

Thank you for your letter of March 10, 1977, re-
questing an opinion concerning whether the Community Center
Authority, a joint powers agency formed pursuant to Government Code Section 6500 et seq., is a local government agency
within the meaning of the Political Reform Act and, if so,
whether it is required to adopt a conflict of interest code
pursuant to Government Code Section 87300. Because your re-
quest does not raise a substantial question of interpretation
under the Act, the Commission will not issue a formal opinion.
See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18320(g)(2). However, I think
the following informal advice will enable you to resolve this
matter.

"Local government agency" is defined in the Political
Reform Act to mean:

a county, city or district of any kind in-
cluding school district, or any other local
or regional political subdivision, or any
department, division, bureau, office, board,
commission or other agency of these, but
does not include any court or any agency in
the judicial branch of government.

Gov. Code §82041

The Community Center Authority formed pursuant to the Joint
Exercise of Powers Agreement entered into between the City of
Anaheim and the Anaheim Union High School District clearly
falls within the ambit of this provision. The Community Center

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL (322-5601 • ENFORCEMENT-CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

William P. Hopkins, Jr.
Page Two
April 12, 1977

Authority was formed to jointly exercise powers common to the
two entities that are parties to the Joint Powers Agreement
and both of the parties are clearly local government agencies.
See Government Code Section 6502. In addition, pursuant to
Government Code Section 6507, the Community Center Authority
itself is a "public entity."

We also think it is readily apparent that the Community Center Authority will have to adopt a conflict of
interest code pursuant to Government Code Section 87300. As
you indicate in your letter, the Authority possesses various
powers, including the power to enter into contracts, to construct and operate an auditorium and exhibition building,
to acquire and dispose of property, and to sue and be sued in
its own name. It is clear, therefore, that the members of the
Authority make governmental decisions which foreseeably may
have a material financial effect on financial interests. See
Government Code Section 87302(a); 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18700(b).

Apparently the City Council of the City of Anaheim
will be the code reviewing body for the Community Center Authority and, hence, has the obligation to establish a deadline
for submission of the Authority's code. See Government Code
Sections 82011, 87303. Nevertheless, it is our conclusion
that the Community Center Authority is a local government
agency and will have to adopt a conflict of interest code.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321.
If you have any further questions, please contact Kenneth Coshorn,
an attorney in the Commission's Conflict of Interest Division.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:KF:jo

sections.question_synthetic: Is the Community Center Authority, a joint powers agency formed between the City of Anaheim and the Anaheim Union High School District, a local government agency under the Political Reform Act, and if so, must it adopt a conflict of interest code?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Community Center Authority is a local government agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act and is required to adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to Government Code Section 87300. The City Council of Anaheim, as the code reviewing body, has the obligation to establish a deadline for the Authority's code submission.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice letter (not a formal opinion) in response to Opinion Request No. 77-005. The document contains the question and conclusion within the body text rather than in explicitly labeled sections. Standard letter format with clear reasoning provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is the Community Center Authority, a joint powers agency formed between the City of Anaheim and the Anaheim Union High School District, a local government agency under the Political Reform Act, and if so, must it adopt a conflict of interest code?

CONCLUSION: The Community Center Authority is a local government agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act and is required to adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to Government Code Section 87300. The City Council of Anaheim, as the code reviewing body, has the obligation to establish a deadline for the Authority's code submission.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Community Center Authority formed as a joint powers agency between the City of Anaheim and the Anaheim Union High School District qualifies as a local government agency and must adopt a conflict of interest code, with the City Council serving as the reviewing body.
